reasoning: Texas generally supports its existing Compassionate Use Program (TCUP) which allows for the sale of low-THC medical cannabis. Given the resolution criteria specify the law must "fully prohibit the sale of THC within the state" and that "partial bans... will not count," any bill that solely targets hemp-derived THC (like Delta-8 or Delta-9) while leaving the medical program intact could arguably be viewed as a partial ban. Even assuming the question intends to ask about a ban on all non-medical hemp-derived THC, previous attempts to enact a total ban have met with stiff resistance from the hemp industry and failed to pass both chambers. With the legislative session ending in late May/early June 2025, there is a relatively low likelihood of such a sweeping prohibition overcoming the necessary legislative hurdles before July 1, 2025.
